title: Per-action-step parallel decomposition of the step-iterating specs (functionality + build delta)
question: Can the per-action-step specs (propose-responsibilities et al.) be decomposed into PARALLEL per-step agent calls, and what exact pi-workflows / bridge / parser functionality supports it versus must be built?
method: code-inspection of the pi-workflows engine (src + dist), the wasc bridges (render_from_substrate.py, apply_from_substrate.py, run_workflow.mjs, apply.py), and the parsers (planner/specs.py); corroborated against the live 14-spec run (propose-responsibilities was the long pole: ~78KB rendered prompt now carrying the 444-atom inventory).
findings_summary: DECOMPOSABLE axis = per action step. The 5 step-iterating specs -- propose-responsibilities, propose-assignments, propose-timelines, propose-step-resources, propose-evidence -- apply via per-step OVERLAY (_set_indexed_field_on_steps, apply.py:83-150: each spec's MERGE_RULE overlays its fields onto action_steps[idx], not list-replace), so per-step PARTIAL results merge cleanly by step_index; each step's mapping is independent. NOT decomposable: bind-measurement-channels (criterion_index<->channel_indices cross-refs the drafted criteria/channels) and propose-review-loop (trigger_index references G1's OWN emitted review_events) -- intra-spec coherence requires one call. The remaining specs are single-output (not step-iterating). ENGINE: forEach (step-foreach.ts) runs SEQUENTIALLY (a for-loop collecting outputs[]; MAX_FOREACH_ITERATIONS=500), so it does NOT itself parallelize; BUT the engine HAS parallel execution -- executeParallelLayer + executeParallelStep (step-parallel.ts:44,84,107,141 via Promise.allSettled + a shared AbortController cancelling siblings on failure). So fan-out is achievable via explicit parallel steps/layers, or by refactoring executeForEach to Promise.all. BRIDGE GAP: render_from_substrate.py renders the WHOLE spec prompt (argv --spec-key/--seed/--draft-path, no step scoping; :43-64,:109) -- per-step needs a --step-index + template scoping. The per-step APPLY merge already supports partials (reuse). COHERENCE: the shared preamble's all-divisions mandate (every division must have a role) is a WHOLE-PLAN constraint no single per-step call sees -> a post-merge coverage/synthesis pass is required. CACHING: dispatch.ts sets up none (cacheRead/Write are populated from pi output, no --cache-key); each parallel call re-sends the full preamble + inventory. BUILD DELTA: (a) per-step render variant (--step-index + snippet section scoping); (b) a fan-out+merge workflow shape (explicit parallel steps or a parallelForEach refactor); (c) a coverage/synthesis pass for the all-divisions constraint; (d) optional prompt caching. EXISTS to reuse: engine parallel-step execution, the per-step overlay merges, state/resume. Most valuable for the production orchestration trio (DEC-29 north-star); a wall-clock optimization for the workshop (not blocking -- the full sequential run completed).

citations.label: per-step overlay merge used by the 5 step-iterating specs

stale_conditions: pi-workflows changes forEach to parallel, or changes the parallel-step primitives
apply.py MERGE_RULES stop using per-step overlay for the step-iterating specs
a step-iterating spec's parser gains intra-spec cross-references
